page: Google Cloud Platform z3-highmem-14-standardlssd is a memory-optimized server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor with x86_64 architecture. It features 14 dedicated vCPUs derived from 7 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The system is equipped with 112 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core. Storage is bundled with a single local NVMe SSD offering 3221 GB of capacity, while no GPUs are present.

Benchmark results place this instance in the average performance tier across multiple workloads. The Intel Xeon processor achieves a single-core stress-ng score of 1623.08 and a multi-core score of 11635.06, supported by an L3 cache of 105 MB. Memory performance is characterized by a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 951.78 GB/sec, which drops to 85.51 GB/sec for uncached large blocks, and a write bandwidth of 39.89 GB/sec. Database performance is highlighted by a Passmark database operations score of 6737.04 and a Redis SET throughput of 15.53 million operations per second.

The CPU includes advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), which assist in specialized tasks. For instance, the server processes 1181.08 tokens per second for prompt processing on a small 135M LLM, but performance drops to 9.0 tokens per second for prompt processing on a large 70B model, with text generation data unavailable for the 70B model. This indicates a clear tradeoff when attempting large-scale model execution without dedicated GPU acceleration.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the high density of local NVMe storage combined with a generous memory allocation makes this instance a balanced choice for workloads that require fast local scratch space and substantial memory without the overhead of independent storage attachments. It is highly suitable for memory-intensive databases, caching layers like Redis, software compilation, and medium-scale data processing pipelines.
description: The Google Cloud Platform z3-highmem-14-standardlssd is a memory-optimized server configured with 14 dedicated vCPUs on an Intel Xeon 8481C processor, 112 GB of RAM, and a 3221 GB local NVMe SSD. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, this instance features advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel AMX. Benchmark data indicates solid performance for memory-bound and database applications, with a Passmark database score of 6737.04 and Redis SET operations reaching 15.53 million operations per second. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core, combined with substantial local NVMe storage, provides a resource-dense profile for workloads requiring fast local disk access. However, the lack of a GPU limits its utility for large-scale AI inference, as demonstrated by prompt processing speeds dropping to 9.0 tokens per second on a 70B model. This server is best suited for memory-intensive databases, caching layers, and local data processing.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform z3-highmem-14-standardlssd is a memory-optimized server with 14 vCPUs, 112 GB RAM, and a 3221 GB local NVMe SSD, designed for database and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform z3-highmem-14-standardlssd features 14 vCPUs, 112 GB RAM, and a 3221 GB local NVMe SSD for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized server featuring high-capacity local NVMe storage and dedicated Intel Xeon processors for demanding database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 14 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor
112 GB of system memory providing 8 GB per core
Bundled 3221 GB local NVMe SSD for high-speed storage
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction support for accelerated vector math
Optimized for memory-intensive databases, Redis caching, and data processing
